sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The light green square is now at bottom-center [42:43, 10:11]. The level didn't complete.
Let me think about this more carefully. I've been trying to move the light green square to different positions, but the level hasn't completed. Maybe the goal is not about moving the light green square.
Let me re-examine the game. The right shapes have a "stepped" pattern. The left blocks rearrange when I click on the right shapes. The colored squares move when I click on the right shapes or use arrow keys.
Maybe the game is about "painting" the left blocks to match the right shapes. The colored squares are "brushes" that paint the blocks. When I click on a right shape, it "paints" the corresponding left block with that shape's color.
But I haven't seen any evidence of the blocks being painted. The blocks just rearrange when I click on the right shapes.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the game is about matching the colors of the left blocks to the right shapes. The red shape is red, and the blue shape is blue. The left blocks include a red block and a blue block.
Maybe the goal is to have the red block adjacent to the red shape's position on the left side, and the blue block adjacent to the blue shape's position on the left side. The red shape is at the top-right of the right panel, and the blue shape is at the bottom-right. The red block needs to be at the top-right of the left panel, and the blue block needs to be at the bottom-left.
But the current configuration has the red block in the middle and the blue block at the top-right. The red block is not at the top-right.
Wait, let me look at the current configuration again:
- Blue rect: [20:23, 18:21] (4x4) - top-right
- Gray: [30:33, 8:11] (4x4) - middle-left
- Red: [30:33, 12:17] (4x6) - middle-center
- Dark red: [30:33, 18:21] (4x4) - middle-right
- Checkerboard: [34:37, 8:11] (4x4) - bottom-left
The blue block is at the top-right, which matches the red shape's position (top-right of right panel). The red block is in the middle, which doesn't match the blue shape's position (bottom-right of right panel).
Maybe the goal is to swap the blue and red blocks, so the red block is at the top-right and the blue block is at the bottom-left.
But I don't know how to swap the blocks. Clicking on the right shapes rearranges the blocks, but I'm not sure about the exact rules.
Let me try clicking on the red shape and see what happens.
Actually, wait. Let me think about this more carefully. The right shapes are:
- Red shape: top-right of right panel
- Blue shape: bottom-right of right panel
The left blocks are:
- Blue block: top-right of left panel
- Red block: middle of left panel
- Checkerboard: bottom-left of left panel
Maybe the game is about "copying" the right shapes' positions to the left side. The red shape is at the top-right, so the red block should be at the top-right. The blue shape is at the bottom-right, so the blue block should be at the bottom-right (or bottom-left, since the left panel is mirrored).
But the current configuration has the blue block at the top-right and the red block in the middle. This doesn't match the right shapes' positions.
Let me try clicking on the red shape and see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on the red shape to see what happens
predict(expect_change=True, note='Click on red shape to rearrange left blocks')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 18, 'col': 48}])

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== Left blocks after clicking red ===")
for r in range(18, 44):
    line = ascii_lines[r][7:22]
    if any(c not in 'c' for c in line):
        print(f"  {r}: {line}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        r0, r1 = min(p[0] for p in b), max(p[0] for p in b)
        c0, c1 = min(p[1] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"\nLight green: [{r0}:{r1},{c0}:{c1}]")

print(f"\nLevel completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

# Let me also check the right shapes
print("\n=== Right shapes ===")
for r in range(16, 24):
    line = ascii_lines[r][36:56]
    if any(c not in 'B' for c in line):
        print(f"  {r}: {line}")
for r in range(32, 40):
    line = ascii_lines[r][36:56]
    if any(c not in 'B' for c in line):
        print(f"  {r}: {line}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
